req.title: Submit leave request — variant 5
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
preconditions: authenticated
employment_type:full_time
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-061 defines five distinct test-worthy regions: (1) valid leave within 1-30 business days (EP positive), (2) exactly 30-day boundary (BVA boundary), (3) 31-day request without hr-admin approval (NEG/DT negative), (4) overlapping existing leave (NEG negative), and (5) start_date > end_date (NEG negative). Two-factor authentication is a cross-cutting precondition applied to all cases. State transition is relevant because the leave request moves through states: draft -> submitted -> manager-notified. Decision Table covers the hr-admin approval rule. All tests use fixture-based setup/teardown to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_valid_15_business_days
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
User has completed 2FA
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
Target date range spans exactly 15 business days
actions: Fill start_date with a Monday 15 business days before end_date
expected: Leave request record created in DB via <assert_leave_created_fn>
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_submit_leave_request_valid_15_business_days(
    authenticated_full_time_page,
    tfa_helper,
    leave_api_client,
    assert_leave_created,
    assert_manager_notified,
    assert_email_sent,
    business_day_range,
):
    page = authenticated_full_time_page
    start_date, end_date = business_day_range(days=15)

    tfa_helper.complete(page)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start_date.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end_date.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-leave']")

    page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='success-banner']")
    leave_id = page.get_attribute("[data-testid='leave-id']", "value")

    assert_leave_created(leave_id)
    assert_manager_notified(leave_id)
    assert_email_sent(leave_id)

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_business_days_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
User has completed 2FA
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
Target date range spans exactly 30 business days (upper inclusive boundary)
actions: Fill start_date and end_date spanning exactly 30 business days
expected: HTTP 201 or success redirect received (no hr-admin approval required)
trace: R-WEB-061: upper boundary 30 business days requires no hr-admin
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_business_days_boundary(
    authenticated_full_time_page,
    tfa_helper,
    leave_api_client,
    assert_leave_created,
    assert_manager_notified,
    assert_no_hradmin_workflow,
    business_day_range,
):
    page = authenticated_full_time_page
    start_date, end_date = business_day_range(days=30)

    tfa_helper.complete(page)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start_date.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end_date.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-leave']")

    page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='success-banner']")
    leave_id = page.get_attribute("[data-testid='leave-id']", "value")

    assert_leave_created(leave_id)
    assert_manager_notified(leave_id)
    assert_no_hradmin_workflow(leave_id)

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_31_business_days_without_hradmin_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
User has completed 2FA
No hr-admin approval token provided
Target date range spans exactly 31 business days (exceeds limit)
actions: Fill start_date and end_date spanning 31 business days
Submit the form without hr-admin approval
expected: Error message referencing hr-admin approval displayed
trace: R-WEB-061: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin
R-WEB-061: hr-admin approval requirement for >30 day requests
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_submit_leave_request_31_business_days_without_hradmin_returns_422(
    authenticated_full_time_page,
    tfa_helper,
    leave_api_client,
    assert_leave_not_created,
    business_day_range,
):
    page = authenticated_full_time_page
    start_date, end_date = business_day_range(days=31)

    tfa_helper.complete(page)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start_date.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end_date.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-leave']")

    error_banner = page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='error-banner']")
    assert error_banner.is_visible()
    error_text = error_banner.inner_text()
    assert "hr-admin" in error_text.lower() or "approval" in error_text.lower()
    assert_leave_not_created(start_date, end_date)

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_start_date_after_end_date_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
User has completed 2FA
start_date is set to a date after end_date
actions: Fill start_date with a date chronologically after end_date
expected: Validation error message about invalid date range displayed
trace: R-WEB-061: 400 when start_date > end_date error pathway
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_submit_leave_request_start_date_after_end_date_returns_400(
    authenticated_full_time_page,
    tfa_helper,
    assert_leave_not_created,
):
    page = authenticated_full_time_page
    end_date = date.today() + timedelta(days=5)
    start_date = end_date + timedelta(days=3)

    tfa_helper.complete(page)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", start_date.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", end_date.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-leave']")

    error_banner = page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='error-banner']")
    assert error_banner.is_visible()
    error_text = error_banner.inner_text()
    assert "date" in error_text.lower()
    assert_leave_not_created(start_date, end_date)

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_leave_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated as full-time employee
User has completed 2FA
An approved leave request already exists for an overlapping date range
actions: Seed an existing approved leave via <seed_existing_leave_fixture>
Fill start_date and end_date overlapping the seeded leave
expected: Conflict error message referencing overlapping leave displayed
No new leave request record created
trace: R-WEB-061: 409 when overlapping existing leave error pathway
R-WEB-061: state transition from submitted to rejected-conflict
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_leave_returns_409(
    authenticated_full_time_page,
    tfa_helper,
    seed_existing_leave,
    assert_leave_not_created,
):
    page = authenticated_full_time_page
    base = date.today() + timedelta(days=10)
    existing_start = base
    existing_end = base + timedelta(days=4)
    seed_existing_leave(existing_start, existing_end)

    overlap_start = existing_start + timedelta(days=2)
    overlap_end = existing_end + timedelta(days=2)

    tfa_helper.complete(page)
    page.goto("/leave-requests/new")
    page.fill("[data-testid='start-date']", overlap_start.isoformat())
    page.fill("[data-testid='end-date']", overlap_end.isoformat())
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-leave']")

    error_banner = page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='error-banner']")
    assert error_banner.is_visible()
    error_text = error_banner.inner_text()
    assert "overlap" in error_text.lower() or "conflict" in error_text.lower()
    assert_leave_not_created(overlap_start, overlap_end)
